content.cn_text: 你将听到一段关于工作场所设计最新研究的讲座。
content.en_text: You will hear part of a talk about recent research into the design of workplaces.

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间查看第31至40题。
content.en_text: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听，并回答第31至40题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 大家好。
content.en_text: Hello everyone.

content.cn_text: 我最近完成了一项重要研究，研究办公空间的设计方式及其
content.en_text: I recently completed some important research into the way office space is designed and its

content.cn_text: 对员工的影响。
content.en_text: impact on employees.

content.cn_text: 开放式办公室已经成为世界各地办公室的常见形式。
content.en_text: The open plan office had been the norm for offices around the world.

content.cn_text: 我相信你们都能想象出这种办公室。
content.en_text: I'm sure you can picture one.

content.cn_text: 一个大房间里，可能有50或100名员工坐在自己的工位上。
content.en_text: A big room with maybe 50 or 100 workers sitting at their workstations.

content.cn_text: 你可能会认为开放式设计是近来的现象，但它们实际上出现于
content.en_text: You might think open plan designs are a recent phenomenon, but they actually came about

content.cn_text: 20世纪早期，当时企业需要大量人员进行打字工作。
content.en_text: in the early 20th century when businesses needed large numbers of people to do typing.

content.cn_text: 这些员工通常是女性，过去必须成排坐着，在打字机前工作，
content.en_text: These employees, usually women, used to have to sit in rows working away at their type

content.cn_text: 并处在主管的密切监督之下。
content.en_text: writers under the watchful eye of their supervisor.

content.cn_text: 多年来，设计师们提出了几种开放式主题的变体。
content.en_text: Over the years, designers have come up with several variations on the open theme.

content.cn_text: 尤其是通过家具布置实现了这些变化。
content.en_text: In particular, this has been achieved through furnishings.

content.cn_text: 早期设计的特点是使用独立的办公桌。
content.en_text: The early designs featured separate desks.

content.cn_text: 后来的设计引入了隔间的概念。
content.en_text: A subsequent design introduced the concept of the cubicle.

content.cn_text: 这种隔间可以有员工看不到外面的高隔板，也可以有刚好足够低的隔板，
content.en_text: This can have either high screens that workers can't see over or a screen just low enough

content.cn_text: 让员工能留意经理的动向。
content.en_text: to keep a lookout for the manager.

content.cn_text: 如今，随着大型呼叫中心的增加，一种被称为无固定工位制的现象
content.en_text: And nowadays, with the increase in large call centres, a phenomenon known as hot desking

content.cn_text: 很常见。
content.en_text: is common.

content.cn_text: 在这种制度下，不同班次的员工被要求共用同一个工位。
content.en_text: In this system, employees working on different shifts are asked to share the same workstation.

content.cn_text: 可以想象，这并不受员工欢迎，因为他们几乎没有机会
content.en_text: As you can imagine, this is not very popular with staff, as there is little opportunity

content.cn_text: 把自己的工作空间个性化。
content.en_text: to personalise your workspace.

content.cn_text: 事实上，我的研究表明，员工总体上不喜欢开放式设计。
content.en_text: In fact, my research shows that employees in general dislike the open plan design.

content.cn_text: 作为研究的一部分，我对几家大公司的员工进行了调查，
content.en_text: As a part of my research, I conducted a survey of the staff in several large companies

content.cn_text: 这些公司都采用开放式办公室。
content.en_text: with open plan offices.

content.cn_text: 员工们都对这种设计表达了相似的担忧。
content.en_text: The employees all voiced similar concerns about the design.

content.cn_text: 首先，他们觉得开放式办公室剥夺了他们的隐私，但许多人也觉得
content.en_text: Firstly, they felt that the open plan took away their privacy, but many also felt the

content.cn_text: 需要一直随身携带自己的贵重物品。
content.en_text: need to carry their valuables with them at all times.

content.cn_text: 所以在某种程度上，他们也觉得这种设计缺乏安全性。
content.en_text: So to some extent, they also felt there was less security with these designs.

content.cn_text: 另一个常见抱怨也许是雇主在选择这种
content.en_text: Another common complaint may be something for employers to consider when choosing this

content.cn_text: 设计时需要考虑的事情，那就是这类办公空间对健康有害。
content.en_text: type of design, and this was that these types of office space are bad for the health.

content.cn_text: 我对这种可能性感到很感兴趣，而查看员工记录后，这一点似乎确实
content.en_text: I was intrigued by this possibility, but looking at staff records, it does seem to be

content.cn_text: 得到了证实。
content.en_text: borne out.

content.cn_text: 那么，如果这种设计如此不受欢迎，为什么它还如此普遍呢？
content.en_text: So if this design is so unpopular, why is it so commonplace?

content.cn_text: 答案在于雇主认为这种设计
content.en_text: Well, the answer to that is in the benefits that the employers feel this type of design

content.cn_text: 具有一些好处。
content.en_text: has.

content.cn_text: 当然，一个明显的好处是这种设计显著降低成本，既包括
content.en_text: Of course, there is the obvious benefit that this design significantly reduces costs, both

content.cn_text: 租金方面，也包括能源使用方面。
content.en_text: in terms of rent and even in terms of energy use.

content.cn_text: 雇主还认为，开放式设计能带来更好的团队合作和沟通。
content.en_text: Employers also argue that an open plan design leads to better teamwork and communication.

content.cn_text: 他们还认为，新员工可以通过观察其他员工来学习，因此这种设计
content.en_text: They also feel that new staff can learn from observing other workers, so this design

content.cn_text: 实际上有助于培训员工。
content.en_text: can actually help in training staff.

content.cn_text: 然而，员工提出的抱怨似乎表明，这些缺点远远
content.en_text: However, the complaints voiced by employees seem to suggest that the disadvantages far

content.cn_text: 超过了优点。
content.en_text: outweigh the advantages.

content.cn_text: 现实是，不开心的员工不太可能很好地协作，而且有大量
content.en_text: The reality is that unhappy workers are unlikely to work well together, and there is plenty

content.cn_text: 证据表明，员工经常会被周围的噪音分散工作注意力，
content.en_text: of evidence to suggest workers are frequently distracted from their work by the noise that

content.cn_text: 这些噪音无处不在。
content.en_text: is all around them.

content.cn_text: 显而易见的是，作为设计师，我们需要认真思考
content.en_text: What is clear is that, as designers, we need to think carefully about the impact that

content.cn_text: 我们的设计对相关员工以及他们所做工作的影响。
content.en_text: our design has both on the staff involved and the work that they do.

content.cn_text: 第四部分到此结束。
content.en_text: It is the end of Part 4.

content.cn_text: 现在你有一分钟时间检查第四部分的答案。
content.en_text: You now have one minute to check your answers to Part 4.

groups.desc:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.title: Questions 31–40

items.title: workers employed to do 31. __________ were arranged in rows
items.analysis: 答案解析：第31题问早期20世纪员工被雇来做什么，原文说 do typing，因此答案是 typing。

items.title: first, workers had individual 32. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第32题问最初员工有 individual _______，原文说 separate desks，因此答案是 desks。

items.title: later, they had cubicles with high or low 33. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第33题问 cubicles with high or low _______，原文说 high screens / a screen，因此答案是 screens。

items.title: nowadays, workers may have to 34. __________ a work area
items.analysis: 答案解析：第34题问 nowadays workers may have to _______ a work area，原文说 share the same workstation，因此答案是 share。

items.title: they pose a risk to our 37. __________ (staff records support this)
items.analysis: 答案解析：第37题问开放式设计对什么构成风险，且员工记录支持这一点，原文说 bad for the health，并说 staff records 证实，因此答案是 health。

items.title: they significantly reduce rent and 38. __________ costs
items.analysis: 答案解析：第38题问 reduce rent and _______ costs，原文说 rent and energy use，因此答案是 energy。

items.title: they can make 39. __________ staff easier
items.analysis: 答案解析：第39题问这种设计可以让 _______ staff easier，原文说 help in training staff，因此答案是 training。

items.title: In reality, employees are unhappy and they are often distracted by the 40. __________ in the office.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第40题问员工常被办公室里的什么分心，原文说 distracted by the noise，因此答案是 noise。

groups.source_collect:

Office Design

1960s to present
Variations in design of open-plan offices:
• first, workers had individual $32$
• later, they had cubicles with high or low $33$
• nowadays, workers may have to $34$ a work area
Survey results
Attitudes of employees to open-plan designs:
• there is a lack of $35$ and $36$
• they pose a risk to our $37$ (staff records support this)
Attitudes of employers to open-plan designs:
• they significantly reduce rent and $38$ costs
• they improve teamwork and communication
• they can make $39$ staff easier
In reality, employees are unhappy and they are often distracted by the $40$ in the office.
